summarization.a: Schematic illustration of the MOS capacitor structure consisting of Ni / HfO₂ (24 Å) / [Al₂O₃ (nΔÅ) cycles] / GeOₓ (5 Å) / p-Ge(100).
summarization.b: It is a line chart representing the Capacitance–voltage (C–V) measured  at a frequency of 1.5MHz for ALD of 9 cycles
summarization.c: It is a line chart representing the Gate current density–voltage (JG – V) characteristics for MOS capacitors with n = 9 cycles of Al₂O₃ ALD with a flat band voltage of 1 V.
summarization.d: It is a line chart representing the Maximum capacitance (C_max) extracted from C–V measurements which was recorded at f = 1.5 MHz.
summarization.e: It is a line chart representing the Gate leakage current density (J_G) measured at 1 V overdrive with respect to the flatband voltage (V = V_FB − 1 V).
summarization.f: It is a line chart representing the C–V hysteresis (ΔV_FB) evaluated at the flatband voltage.
summarization.g: It is a line chart representing the Interface trap density (D_it) at the valence band edge (E = E_V), extracted from frequency-dependent C–V measurements using the Castagne–Vapaille method.

d.question: Based on panels d & e, how does increasing Al₂O₃ cycles (n) affect the maximum capacitance and gate leakage? Explain the cause.
d.answer: Increasing *n* decreases Cₘₐₓ and significantly reduces J_G. This is caused by the increased total physical thickness and improved dielectric quality of the bilayer, which increases the effective oxide thickness (EOT) and creates a better barrier against electron tunneling.

f.question: Given the reduced hysteresis (ΔV_FB) and low D_it at *n=9* (labels f & g), is this stack suitable for a non-volatile memory charge-trapping layer?
f.answer: No. Non-volatile memory requires significant, stable hysteresis for charge storage. The low ΔV_FB shown here indicates minimal charge trapping, which is excellent for logic transistors but undesirable for memory function.

e.question: Compare the device performance at 9 ALD cycles versus 15 ALD cycles by analyzing graphs (d) and (e). While the 15-cycle sample offers superior leakage reduction (J_G), why would the 9-cycle sample likely be defined as the optimal engineering 'sweet spot' for a high-speed logic application?"
e.answer: For high-speed logic, 9 cycles is the optimal choice. Transistor switching speed and drive current depend directly on Gate Capacitance (C_{ox}). The 9-cycle sample maximizes this speed metric (C_{max}) while providing sufficient passivation (D_{it} saturation, graph g), whereas the 15-cycle sample sacrifices too much performance (speed) for a marginal gain in leakage reduction.

b.question: What is the purpose of the fixed 9 cycles of Al₂O₃ ALD in panels b & c, and what does it help establish?
b.answer: It serves as a benchmark process condition to evaluate the electrical properties (C-V and J-V) of a specific, intermediate gate stack thickness. This establishes a baseline for comparing the trends shown in the subsequent labels (d-g).
